evals.name: shadcn-ui-minimal-button
evals.prompt:
I have a Figma design system with just a Button component following shadcn/ui patterns. It has 6 variants (primary, secondary, destructive, outline, ghost, link) and 3 sizes (sm, default, lg). The file URL is: https://www.figma.com/design/5nGs36szQAw5jZAOGYkBcV/-shadcn-ui---Design-System--Community-?node-id=2-287&p=f&t=ktBm2TF4AbZZcc90-0

Generate /specs/ for this system. I want to see how well it extracts tokens, documents the Button component, and validates shadcn/ui compliance.
evals.expected_output:
Complete /specs/ directory with:
- tokens/ (colors, typography, spacing, radius extracted from Figma variables)
- components/Button.md (all 6 variants × 3 sizes documented with props, states, token bindings)
- design-decisions.md (template with TODOs)
- conventions.md (shadcn/ui compliance validation)
- exports/style-dictionary.json (W3C Design Token format)
- High shadcn/ui compliance score (>90%)
- 8-point audit results for Button component

assertions.description: Style Dictionary export is valid JSON

evals.name: shadcn-ui-full-system-2026
evals.prompt:
Audit this comprehensive shadcn/ui design system - The Ultimate UI Kit with 100+ components: https://www.figma.com/design/YKREgYnZBLQBUEWG2o4vei/shadcn-ui--The-Ultimate-UI-Kit-for-Figma--Community-?node-id=0-1&p=f&t=TPw1fHbd8oLQ4UQv-0

This should have multiple components (Button, Input, Select, Checkbox, etc.), proper token architecture with variables, and comprehensive component coverage.

Generate complete /specs/ and tell me the compliance score.
evals.expected_output:
Complete /specs/ directory with:
- tokens/ (colors with light/dark modes, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, motion)
- components/ (10+ component files: Button.md, Input.md, Select.md, Checkbox.md, etc.)
- Each component has full 8-point audit results
- design-decisions.md and conventions.md generated
- exports/style-dictionary.json with multi-mode support
- Comprehensive audit report showing:
  - Total tokens extracted (colors, typography, spacing, etc.)
  - Total components documented
  - Convention compliance score
  - Any violations found
  - Component readiness table

evals.name: validation-reporting-test
evals.prompt:
Test the validation capabilities: Audit this 2026 design system with Figma Variables and specifically check for:
1. shadcn/ui convention violations (wrong prop names, wrong variant names)
2. Hardcoded colors instead of variables
3. Missing states (hover, focus, disabled)
4. Touch target violations (<44px)
5. Accessibility issues (contrast, focus rings)

File: https://www.figma.com/design/3jJQE9SGgraMP87rGS7Xfo/Shadcn-ui-Figma-Library-%E2%80%93-UI-Kit--Components--Design-System--2026---Community-?node-id=5984-59776&p=f&t=a6SUrv69FbPjZ695-0

Even if the system is compliant, I want to see how you report validation results.
evals.expected_output:
Comprehensive audit report clearly showing:
- Convention compliance score with breakdown
- Violations flagged by category (if any):
  - Prop naming violations
  - Variant naming violations  
  - Token binding violations (hardcoded values)
  - Missing states per component
  - Touch target failures
  - Accessibility issues
- Generated /specs/ with violations documented in:
  - conventions.md (all violations listed)
  - Each component .md file (Known Issues section populated)
- If no violations found, clear statement of 100% compliance
